concerns.text: Tasks T032 and T039 implement 'rule-space enumeration' (Wolfram review) which is not authorized by spec.md or plan.md. The spec only requires sensitivity analysis (FR-007). This is unapproved scope creep; no task exists for the required FR-007 sensitivity analysis sweep, only for the unauthorized enumeration.

Task T036 instructs modifying `spec.md` to define the 'sensitization' metric. The spec is a requirements artifact, not an implementation target. The metric is defined in SC-002. This task misdirects the implementer and fails to provide a task for the actual implementation of the sample efficiency measurement logic required by SC-002/US-2.

Tasks T035, T037, and T038 target `docs/research_notes.md` and `docs/design.md`. The `plan.md` Project Structure does not define these files (only `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `quickstart.md`). These tasks reference non-existent plan elements, creating a coverage gap for the required documentation of the dendritic model (Constitution VI) and logical calculus.

FR-007 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping the dendritic nonlinearity threshold (low/moderate/high). While T029 mentions 'sensitivity analysis', it is buried in US-3 implementation without explicit linkage to the FR-007 requirement or the specific 'sweep' logic mandated. The unauthorized T032/T039 tasks crowd out the explicit FR-007 coverage.
